docs.title: Career Target
docs.text: Finance Business Partner / Business Controller FMCG Industry

docs.title: Pattern 1
docs.text:
Concept → Explanation → Example → Impact

Example:

The system helps people understand the cost.

docs.title: Better
docs.text:
Impact → Reason → Evidence

Example:

The new costing structure enabled teams to identify key cost drivers and make more informed pricing decisions.

docs.title: Principle 1
docs.text:
Reality before Concept

Weak:

We improved operational efficiency.

Strong:

Machine utilization increased from 72% to 85%, improving operational efficiency.

docs.title: Principle 2
docs.text:
Evidence before Conclusion

Weak:

The pricing strategy was ineffective.

Strong:

Win rates declined from 35% to 22%, indicating the pricing strategy was ineffective.

docs.title: Principle 3
docs.text:
Impact Landing

Never stop at activity.

Always finish with business impact.

Weak:

I developed a costing system.

Strong:

I developed a costing system, improving cost visibility and supporting more accurate pricing decisions.

docs.title: Principle 4
docs.text:
Executive Brevity

One idea.

One sentence.

One impact.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text: X was driven by Y

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Revenue growth was driven by stronger customer demand.

Margin improvement was driven by a more favorable product mix.

Cost savings were driven by process optimization initiatives.

docs.title: Personal Example
docs.text: Gross margin improvement was driven by better visibility into production cost drivers.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text: By doing X, I was able to Y.

docs.title: Why It Works
docs.text:
This structure highlights ownership and business impact.

Instead of describing activities, it shows what was achieved.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
By automating the monthly reporting process, I was able to reduce preparation time by 80%.

By improving cost allocation logic, I was able to increase costing accuracy across all product categories.

By embedding myself in the factory, I was able to identify operational inefficiencies and improve cost visibility.

docs.title: My Story
docs.text:
By embedding myself in the factory for a month alongside operators and the management team, I was able to map key production processes into the ERP system.

By doing so, I was able to provide detailed visibility into factory capacity and machine utilization, supporting more informed commercial and operational decisions.

docs.title: Structure
docs.text: With X in place, we could Y.

docs.title: Why It Works
docs.text:
Senior leaders care about capability creation.

This frame focuses on what became possible after an initiative was implemented.

It shifts the conversation from activity to business enablement.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
With the new reporting system in place, we could monitor business performance on a daily basis.

With standardized costing rules in place, we could improve pricing consistency across customers.

With better demand forecasting in place, we could optimize inventory levels and reduce working capital.

docs.title: My Story
docs.text:
With detailed production routing and machine time captured in the ERP system, we could gain full visibility into factory capacity.

With this transparency in place, commercial and operational teams could distinguish whether cost variances were driven by technical processes or capacity utilization.

As a result, the business could make more informed pricing decisions and better protect margins during quotation discussions.

docs.title: Frame 4 – Closing Impact
docs.text: Coming soon...

docs.title: Situation
docs.text:
The CEO requested an urgent analysis to explain why internal yarn costs were significantly higher than market prices.

The analysis was needed within three hours.

docs.title: Task
docs.text: I had to identify the major cost gaps, validate assumptions, and provide management with actionable insights under severe time pressure.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
I gathered information from sourcing, costing, and production teams.

I broke the gap into individual components including:

  • Raw material cost
  • Waste rates
  • Processing cost
  • Capacity utilization
  • Overhead allocation

I prioritized the largest drivers and focused the analysis on areas with the highest financial impact.

docs.title: Result
docs.text:
The analysis was completed within the deadline and provided management with a clear understanding of the major cost drivers.

The discussion shifted from assumptions to evidence-based decision-making.

Management was able to focus on the most significant improvement opportunities.

docs.title: Key Interview Message
docs.text: I can provide high-quality financial insight under pressure.

docs.title: Situation
docs.text:
During a factory review, I noticed that product costs were increasing despite stable operational spending.

Management initially believed that manufacturing costs were out of control.

docs.title: Task
docs.text: I needed to identify the real driver behind the cost increase and explain the financial impact to management.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
I analyzed production volume, fixed cost allocation, and capacity utilization across production lines.

I compared actual production levels against standard capacity assumptions and evaluated the impact on fixed cost absorption.

docs.title: Result
docs.text:
The analysis showed that the increase in unit cost was primarily caused by lower production volume rather than higher spending.

This helped management understand that the issue was a capacity utilization problem rather than a cost control problem.

The finding enabled more effective operational actions and prevented incorrect cost reduction initiatives.

docs.title: Key Interview Message
docs.text: I focus on identifying root causes rather than reporting symptoms.

docs.title: Situation
docs.text:
Management observed fluctuations in gross margin but lacked a structured explanation of the underlying drivers.

Different departments often had conflicting interpretations of performance.

docs.title: Task
docs.text: I was responsible for creating a clear bridge between standard gross margin and actual settled gross margin.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
I developed a gross margin bridge that isolated the impact of:

  • Volume
  • Price
  • Product Mix
  • Material Cost
  • Manufacturing Efficiency
  • Settlement Adjustments

I worked with multiple functions to validate each component and ensure consistency.

docs.title: Result
docs.text:
Management gained a transparent understanding of margin movements.

The bridge became a common framework for performance reviews and improved the quality of business discussions.

Decision-making became faster because stakeholders focused on drivers rather than debating numbers.

docs.title: Key Interview Message
docs.text: I transform financial data into business insights.

docs.title: Situation
docs.text:
Monthly reporting required significant manual effort and consumed valuable finance resources.

The process was slow and prone to human error.

docs.title: Task
docs.text: I wanted to automate the reporting process and improve reporting efficiency.

docs.title: Actions
docs.text:
I redesigned the reporting workflow using:

  • Excel Power Query
  • Power BI
  • Power Automate

I standardized data sources, automated refresh processes, and created dynamic dashboards for management reporting.

docs.title: Result
docs.text:
Reporting preparation time was reduced from several days to a matter of minutes.

Management gained faster access to business information and the finance team could spend more time on analysis rather than data preparation.

docs.title: Key Interview Message
docs.text: I use automation to increase the strategic value of finance.

docs.title: Standard Costing System Across 6 Production Cost Centres
Situation
docs.text:
The company lacked a standardized costing structure across multiple production cost centres.

As a result, cost visibility was limited and commercial teams had difficulty understanding the true drivers of product costs.

docs.title: Action
docs.text:
By embedding myself in the factory for a month alongside operators and the management team, I was able to map key production processes and operational drivers into the ERP system.

I worked closely with production, planning, engineering, and finance teams to establish a standardized costing methodology across six production cost centres.

docs.title: Enablement
docs.text:
With detailed routing, machine time, and capacity information captured in the ERP system, we could gain full visibility into production capacity and resource utilization.

With a standardized costing structure in place, commercial and operational teams could work from a single version of the truth when evaluating cost performance.

docs.title: Business Impact
docs.text:
This enabled the business to distinguish whether cost variances were driven by technical processes or capacity utilization issues.

As a result, the company could make more informed pricing decisions, improve quotation accuracy, and better protect gross margins.

docs.title: Key Phrases
docs.text:
• gain full visibility into
  • establish a standardized costing methodology
  • work from a single version of the truth
  • distinguish cost drivers
  • improve quotation accuracy
  • protect gross margins

docs.title: Standard Costing
docs.text:
By embedding myself in the factory for a month, I was able to establish a standardized costing structure across six production cost centers.

With the standard costing system in place, commercial and operations teams could share a common view of cost drivers.

docs.title: Fixed Cost Absorption
docs.text: The analysis showed that the increase in unit cost was primarily caused by lower production volume rather than higher spending.

docs.title: Gross Margin Bridge
docs.text: The bridge provided management with a transparent view of margin movements and their underlying drivers.

docs.title: CEO Cost Gap Analysis
docs.text: I focused the analysis on the largest cost drivers to maximize impact within a limited timeframe.

docs.title: Reporting Automation
docs.text: By automating the reporting process, I was able to significantly reduce preparation time and improve reporting efficiency.
